rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the intended target component or implementation scope
Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting annex blocks
Use valid component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the existing AADL architecture when inserting annex blocks
Ensure AGREE annex blocks are placed in valid scopes for the target component or implementation
Preserve existing subcomponents and connections when inserting implementation-level bindings
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees referring to subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not use subcomponent paths in component type annexes for output dependency contracts
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names are not AGREE variables
Defensive rules about type mismatches in AGREE expressions
